operating_state: Total revenue increased 3% to $15.7 billion driven by commercial aerospace recovery as Collins aftermarket sales and Pratt & Whitney aftermarket increased substantially with improved flight hours and aircraft utilization. Defense sales declined across segments due to supply chain constraints, lower F-35 volume, timing of F-135 awards, and material receipt delays.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved to 20.1% from 17.8% as favorable sales mix and productivity gains offset organic cost increases. Selling, general and administrative expenses increased to 9.3% of sales driven by higher employee-related costs and Russia-related credit loss provisions. Company-funded R&D remained stable at 4.0% of sales while customer-funded R&D decreased due to lower military program activity.
material_events: Reversed $1.3 billion of backlog and recorded $296 million pretax impairment charges related to Russia sanctions affecting Pratt & Whitney and Collins operations. Christopher Calio was appointed Chief Operating Officer with $7 million long-term incentive award while Michael Dumais departed as Executive Vice President receiving merger severance payments. Repurchased $743 million of common stock and paid quarterly dividend of $0.51 per share totaling $745 million.
macro_exposures: Russia sanctions resulted in $296 million pretax charges across customer financing assets, inventory reserves, and contract fulfillment costs. China announced potential countermeasures related to Taiwan Foreign Military Sales but has not imposed sanctions. Turkey supply chain risks persist with sole-sourced aerospace components subject to potential U.S. sanctions. Middle East customer advance payments may become refundable due to regulatory approval uncertainties.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued commercial aerospace recovery driven by fundamental air travel demand though timing of full capacity restoration remains uncertain. Defense bookings reflected normal quarterly fluctuations with timing-dependent award patterns. Board declared increased dividend of $0.55 per share for Q2 2022. Tax payments expected to increase by estimated $2 billion due to research expenditure capitalization requirements.
uncertainty_profile: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating uncertainty around commercial air traffic recovery timeline. Supply chain constraints affecting defense program deliveries and production schedules. Pending regulatory approvals for foreign customer contracts subject to evolving U.S. foreign policy considerations. Research expenditure capitalization tax impact dependent on potential Congressional deferral action.
